errors.action: Correct the highlighted scene setting and retry.
Enable Cloth NeXt on at least one supported Mesh or Curve.
Use a valid common Bake range on all deformables.
Apply topology-changing modifiers or keep evaluated topology constant.
Correct the highlighted material or quality value.

errors.cause: Invalid Pin group or Pin animation
errors.action: Check the Pin vertex group, mode, and animated target topology.
Give all deformables a compatible range and supported configuration.
Restore the named object and do not delete it while Bake is starting.
Use a supported Force on an Empty and verify its animated values.
Repair NaN/Inf coordinates, degenerate geometry, or invalid transforms.
Close stale Bake windows and retry.

errors.cause: Companion bundle missing or failed integrity validation
errors.action: Repair or reinstall the Cloth NeXt extension.

errors.cause: Local Companion transport could not start
errors.action: Close stale Bake windows and retry; check local security software.
Check Windows execution permissions, then repair or reinstall the extension.
Close the existing Bake window and retry.

errors.cause: Companion window was not visible or topmost
errors.action: Restore normal desktop/window access and retry.
Close all Bake windows and reinstall the matching extension build.
Check evaluated geometry and the cache folder, then retry.
Choose a writable cache location and check free disk space.
Check modifiers, transforms, and evaluated object geometry.

errors.cause: PPF scene or parameter encoding failed
errors.action: Check geometry, materials, Pins, Forces, and finite numeric values.
Keep pinned topology and objects unchanged throughout the Bake range.
Free disk space or move the cache to a larger writable volume.
Retry after other heavy jobs finish; restart Blender if thread creation keeps failing.

errors.cause: Interrupted or stale partial Bake state
errors.action: Clear the partial result or Rebake; the last complete cache remains safe.
Run the solver health check and repair the managed solver.

errors.cause: Solver executable or runtime dependency missing
errors.action: Repair the managed solver installation.

errors.cause: Solver protocol, schema, or package is incompatible
errors.action: Install the solver version required by this Cloth NeXt release.
Repair the managed solver and inspect its startup diagnostics.
Inspect the solver stderr tail; update GPU drivers or repair dependencies.
Allow the signed solver executable and retry.
Check the local solver connection and retry.

errors.cause: Solver connection or response timed out
errors.action: Retry and check whether security software blocks localhost traffic.
Retry after the solver health check succeeds.

errors.cause: Solver rejected or did not acknowledge upload
errors.action: Inspect the diagnostic log and verify the matching solver version.

errors.cause: Uploaded payload hash or identity mismatch
errors.action: Repair the solver installation and retry with a fresh Bake.
Repair or update the solver to the supported protocol version.
Inspect scene geometry and the solver diagnostic log.
Inspect geometry, materials, Pins, and Forces in the diagnostic log.
Simplify the scene or increase stability/performance headroom, then retry.
Repair intersections, degenerate faces, and invalid collision geometry.
Wait for the owned solver to stop, then retry.
Inspect the failing frame and solver diagnostic log.
Lower Friction first. If it still fails, reduce Collision Gap, increase animated Collider sampling, then try a smaller Time Step.
Separate intersecting geometry at the first Bake frame.
Inspect the last frame, reduce scene complexity, and retry.
Inspect the solver stderr tail and verify GPU/driver stability.
Reduce Time Step and extreme Forces/stiffness; repair invalid input geometry.
Lower scene complexity or raise the RAM Auto Cancel threshold cautiously.

errors.cause: Solver completed without every requested frame
errors.action: Keep the diagnostic log and retry after a solver health check.
Reduce animated Force magnitude and extreme material parameters.
Stop other solver work and retry with a fresh owned project.
Check disk space and the local solver connection.
Check disk performance and localhost security software, then retry.
Retry after a successful solver health check.

errors.cause: Invalid or missing solver output map
errors.action: Keep the diagnostic log and repair/update the solver.
Retry; report the diagnostic log if the same frame is missing again.

errors.cause: Result frame is corrupt or cannot be decoded
errors.action: Check disk integrity and repair/update the solver.

errors.cause: Result size or vertex count mismatch
errors.action: Restore the original topology and Rebake.
Check cache access and target topology.

errors.cause: Playback cache could not be written
errors.action: Choose a writable cache folder with sufficient free space.
Check disk space and filesystem reliability, then Rebake.
Restore the topology used when the Bake began and Rebake.
Restore the target object and Rebake.
Remove conflicting cache modifiers or animation and retry.
Do not use the partial cache; Rebake to create a verified result.

errors.cause: Multi-object cache set is incomplete or inconsistent
errors.action: Keep every target object unchanged and Rebake the full set.

errors.cause: Rod Curve playback could not be applied
errors.action: Restore the original Curve topology and remove conflicting Curve animation.
Wait for cleanup; restart Blender only if the owned process remains stuck.

errors.cause: Cancellation did not complete in time
errors.action: Wait briefly, then restart Blender if the owned solver remains active.

errors.cause: Owned solver process could not be stopped
errors.action: Close Blender after preserving diagnostics, then retry.

errors.cause: Temporary or partial files could not be removed
errors.action: Close programs using the cache folder and clear the partial result.

errors.cause: Bake worker stopped without a terminal result
errors.action: Preserve diagnostics and restart Blender before retrying.
Preserve the diagnostic log and report the code and full Blender error.
